title: Invert Binary Tree
problem_statement: Invert a binary tree by swapping each node’s left and right children, producing the mirror image of the original tree.

examples.explanation: The left and right subtrees are swapped at every node.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 100]
intuition: Invert the tree by swapping child pointers at each node, either recursively or iteratively with a queue.

approaches.name: Recursive inversion
approaches.description: Recursively invert left and right subtrees, then swap them at the current node.
approaches.why_not: An iterative approach can avoid recursion depth but has similar complexity.
interview_commentary: Invert subtrees recursively and then swap them at the current node.
Recursive inversion is simple and mirrors the tree structure naturally.
Null nodes are base cases and stop recursion.

approaches.name: Iterative BFS inversion
approaches.description: Use a queue to traverse the tree and swap children at each visited node iteratively.
approaches.why_not: Recursion is usually simpler and uses less explicit auxiliary data.
interview_commentary: Use BFS to invert nodes level by level if recursion is undesirable.
Swapping children at each node mirrors the tree iteratively.
The queue holds nodes still to process.

interview_qa.q: What operation is performed at each node?
interview_qa.a: Swap the left and right children.
It inverts subtrees first, then swaps them to mirror the current node.

interview_qa.q: How can you do this iteratively?
interview_qa.a: Use BFS or DFS with a stack/queue and swap children for each visited node.

interview_qa.q: What does the tree become after inversion?
interview_qa.a: A mirror image of the original tree.
